pilot_signal_strength.evidence: Activation patching: p=6.3e-05, d=0.87 (large effect). Cross-domain variation: Kruskal-Wallis p=0.005. Layer-dependent pattern: 2-5% at L6/L18 vs 25-35% at L24. H2' (semantic > syntactic) undermined at L24 but hierarchy variation confirmed.

hypothesis_survival.evidence: H7 CONFIRMED (p=6.3e-05). H1 CONFIRMED (p=0.005). H2' NOT CONFIRMED at L24. H3 directional only. H4 REFUTED (rho=0.116). H5 NOT SUPPORTED. H6 NOT CONFIRMED (p=0.87). 2 strong + 1 directional out of 7.

path_to_full_result.evidence: All major experiments complete. RAVEL probe ceiling at F1~0.84 is a genuine limitation, not fixable with more layers. Narrative pivot from 'semantic > syntactic' to 'hierarchy/layer-dependent' is more robust. Remaining work is consolidation and writing.

novelty.evidence: Cross-domain characterization 9/10 (uncontested). Causal patching 8/10. Tightened hedging 8/10. Architecture comparison 7/10. All survive empirical results.

resource_efficiency.evidence: All GPU experiments complete. Remaining work is CPU-only consolidation and writing. Marginal cost ~0.

cand_controlled_dictionary.note: Backup. High novelty (9/10), 1-2 GPU-hours. Not needed -- front runner has sufficient material.

cand_ecological_theory.note: Backup. Theoretical framing. Could supplement but not required.

cand_absorption_aware_correction.note: Backup. Practical contribution. Not needed for current scope.

reasons: Activation patching provides statistically significant causal evidence for absorption (p=6.3e-05, d=0.87, 25 words x 200 contexts). This resolves the critical weakness from the prior REFINE decision (n=7, p=1.0).
Cross-domain absorption rates differ significantly across hierarchy types (Kruskal-Wallis p=0.005). The characterization is novel -- no prior work measures absorption on knowledge hierarchies.
Layer-dependent absorption (2-5% at L6/L18 vs 25-35% at L24) is a genuinely novel finding that reframes where absorption occurs in the residual stream.
GAS definitively fails as absorption detector (rho=0.116 with 25x more data, AUROC=0.571). No further investigation needed.
RAVEL probe quality plateaus at F1~0.84 despite 4 layers tested. This is a ceiling, not a fixable gap. Waiting for F1>=0.90 would be an infinite loop.
Tightened hedging (strict 7.4% vs loose 92.6%) is independently valuable as a methodological contribution.
All major experiments are complete. Remaining work is consolidation and writing. Cost to proceed is negligible.
The H2' reversal (first-letter NOT lowest at L24) changes the narrative but does not invalidate the paper. The revised framing (hierarchy/layer-dependent) is more robust.

narrative_revision_required.old_headline: Semantic hierarchies show MORE absorption than syntactic first-letter
narrative_revision_required.new_headline: Absorption is hierarchy-dependent and layer-dependent, with no simple ordering. First-letter is not representative.
narrative_revision_required.reason: At L24 with improved probes, first-letter (34.5%) is comparable to city-continent (35.8%) and HIGHER than city-country (18.5%) and city-language (13.6%). The prior pilot finding was driven by probe quality artifacts at L12.

next_actions: Run Phase 4.2 consolidation: aggregate all results with updated hypothesis verdicts
Revise proposal.md abstract and framing to reflect hierarchy/layer-dependent narrative
Expand hedging decomposition to L24 across all hierarchies
Compute Absorption Tax T(G) at L24 per hierarchy for qualitative ranking test
Begin paper writing: lead with activation patching (cleanest), then cross-domain characterization
Elevate probe quality ceiling (F1~0.84 for knowledge hierarchies) as methodological finding

positive_results.evidence: First-letter: 2.4% at L6 to 34.5% at L24 (8 SAE configs)

negative_results.result: CMI does not predict absorption at L0=22
H2' (semantic > syntactic) not confirmed at L24

negative_results.evidence: Kruskal-Wallis p=0.87. No architecture dominates across hierarchies.

sanity_checks.sunk_cost_bias_check: Decision based on current evidence quality (p=6.3e-05 for patching, p=0.005 for cross-domain), not on prior investment of 10 iterations.
sanity_checks.note: This is no longer a pilot decision -- full-mode experiments are complete. The ADVANCE threshold (3.5) is met by the strength of causal and cross-domain evidence.
